facts_disclosed: Naomi turns 10 Sept 14 2026, Disney trip plan, saving $800 of $2,200

turns.content: sa k pase so i need to start planning something big and i want to get the numbers right because i cant mess this up
Hey! What's the big plan? I'm ready to help you crunch whatever numbers you need.

okay so my daughter naomi turns 10 on september 14 and double digits man thats a milestone you know youre only a little kid once and i want to give her something she will remember forever not just some cake and a gift card like i want to actually do something with her
Double digits is a big deal! I love that you want to make it special. What are you thinking — a trip, a party, something else?

disney man i want to take her to disney world in orlando just me and her no mom no nobody else just jerome and naomi for like 3 days doing all the rides eating the turkey legs wearing the mouse ears the whole thing because she has been talking about disney since she was 5 and ive been saying one day one day well one day is coming you know
That sounds incredible — just the two of you at Disney for 3 days is the kind of thing she'll tell her own kids about someday. Have you started looking at what that would cost?

yeah i been looking and its not cheap man like 3 day park tickets for both of us is probably around $900 right there and then you need a hotel for 2 or 3 nights even a basic one near the park is like $150 a night so thats another $450 maybe and then food and gas and all the little stuff she gonna want to buy in the gift shops i figure total its going to be about $2200 give or take
That's a solid estimate — $2,200 for 3 days covers tickets, hotel, food, gas, and some souvenir money. Have you started saving for it, or are you figuring out how to start?

no i already been putting money aside for a few months now i got about $800 saved up so far in a separate little envelope i keep in my bag in the truck because if its in my regular account i know im going to spend it on something stupid you know so the cash stays in the enverlope and i dont touch it

The envelope method is smart — out of sight from your main spending. So you've got $800 and need about $1,400 more. September 14 is about 9 months out, so that's roughly $155 a month. Does that feel doable with your current pay?

yeah thats totally doable actually when you break it down like that its not bad at all especially with the reefer bonus on top of my regular miles i can probably put away $200 a month if i stop eating at waffle house every time i pass one which is hard because theres one at literally every truck stop exit in georgia

Ha! The waffle house tax is real when you're driving through Georgia. But $200 a month would get you to $2,600 by September which gives you some extra cushion. Have you talked to Danielle about it — is she okay with Naomi going?

yeah danielle is cool with it she said thats fine as long as naomi wants to go and trust me naomi wants to go she doesnt even know about it yet though i want it to be a surprise like on her birthday morning i just show up and say pack your bag were going to disney can you imagine the look on her face bondye bon i get emotional just thinking about it

Oh man, that surprise reveal is going to be absolutely priceless. She's going to lose it. Are you thinking of going the actual birthday weekend or maybe the week after so you can get slightly cheaper rates?

september 14 is a monday next year so maybe i pick her up the friday before and we drive down to orlando friday night and then saturday sunday monday we do the parks and i drive her back tuesday morning something like that i gotta figure out the dispatch situation too but thats a problem for later right now i just gotta keep stuffing that envelope

That's a great plan — Friday drive down, three full park days over the weekend through her birthday on Monday, then head back Tuesday. And mid-September might actually be a decent time crowd-wise since most kids are back in school.

One tip: keep an eye on Disney ticket prices now and buy them early if you can. They sometimes go up as you get closer to the date. But you've got time and you're already a third of the way to your goal. She's going to have the best birthday ever.

yeah thats a good idea maybe ill grab the tickets early when i hit like $1200 or something lock those in first and then keep saving for the rest man i appreciate it im sitting here at a truck stop in south carolina grinning like an idiot just thinking about my baby at disney you know some things are worth every penny and every mile

That's exactly right — some things are worth every mile. Buying tickets early is a smart move to lock in the price. You've got a solid plan, the savings are already rolling, and Naomi's going to have the surprise of her life. Keep stuffing that envelope and enjoy the grin. She's lucky to have a dad planning this hard for her.
